canonical_question: What are SaaS LTV:CAC ratio benchmarks by company stage - when is it unsustainable vs underinvesting?
aliases: lifetime value to customer acquisition cost ratio
customer lifetime value vs acquisition cost
constraints: Requires accurate, fully-loaded CAC (include salaries, tools, overhead) — most companies undercount by 20-40%
LTV calculation depends on churn rate accuracy; early-stage companies with <12 months of data produce unreliable LTV estimates
Benchmarks vary significantly by sales motion (PLG vs enterprise) and customer segment (SMB vs mid-market vs enterprise)
Ratio alone is misleading without payback period — a 5:1 ratio with 36-month payback can still kill a cash-constrained company
B2C SaaS ratios are structurally lower (median 2.5:1) than B2B (median 4:1) — do not cross-apply benchmarks

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs overall SaaS metrics dashboard (MRR, churn, NRR, burn rate)
User needs CAC benchmarks by channel or sales motion only
User needs churn rate benchmarks specifically

often_confused_with.label: SaaS CAC Payback Period (related but distinct metric)

inputs_needed.question: What stage is the company (seed, Series A, Series B, growth, scale)?
What is the primary sales motion?
What is the primary customer segment?
